mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated IBM Cloud services. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM Cloud infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the IBM Cloud CLI, which is necessary to interact with IBM Cloud services.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Login to IBM Cloud
steps.description:
Authenticate your IBM Cloud account to access the Kubernetes service.

```bash

ibmcloud login

```

> You will be prompted to enter your IBM Cloud credentials.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --kube-version 1.30 --num-workers 2

```

> Adjust the zone and number of workers as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see your nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IBM-Cloud/kube-samples/main/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IBM-Cloud/kube-samples/main/monitoring.yaml

```

> This applies an example autoscaler configuration.
> This applies an example monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> This will delete the cluster without confirmation.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are cleaned up.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete <service-instance-name> --force

```

> Replace <service-instance-name> with the name of your service instance.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. Then, upgrade the cluster and verify the upgrade.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command upgrades your cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Your user account may not have the necessary permissions to perform the requested action.

**Fix:**
Check your IAM roles and ensure you have the required permissions for Kubernetes operations.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the nodes are still initializing or if there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl describe nodes' to identify issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified cluster name may be incorrect or the cluster may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster name with 'ibmcloud ks clusters' and ensure it exists.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity Admission and improved scheduling capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs related to networking.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure all workloads are compatible with the new PodSecurity standards.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
